label: Ambiguous query (disambiguation behavior)
prompt: Search for 'Python release'. Tell me: how did you interpret this query? Did you search for the programming language, the animal, or both? Describe what you found and how you resolved the ambiguity.
interpreted_response:
When you searched for "Python release," I interpreted your query as seeking information about the latest release of the Python programming language. This interpretation is based on the context of our previous discussions and the prominence of the Python programming language in technology and software development.

**Python Programming Language Release:**

The Python Software Foundation supports multiple versions of the Python programming language, including Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, and 3.14. Python 3.15 is currently in the alpha development phase, with a stable release expected in October 2026. The Python community follows an annual release cycle, with each major version receiving two years of full support followed by three years of security support. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_%28programming_language%29?utm_source=openai))

**Python (Animal) Release:**

The term "Python release" can also refer to the release of the snake species into the wild. For example, in Florida, authorities have been deploying robot rabbits to lure invasive Burmese pythons out of hiding, aiding in their capture and removal. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/4cf0b3879d8fa0940178f1262bf84d11?utm_source=openai))

To resolve the ambiguity, I focused on the context of our previous conversations and the prominence of the Python programming language. If you were referring to the animal, please let me know, and I can provide more detailed information on that topic.

citations.title: Robot rabbits the latest tool in Florida battle to control invasive Burmese pythons in Everglades
